PART V

WALKER, Robert; born 1st May 1924; educated Sowerby Bridge Secondary School 1935–42 and Peterhouse, Cambridge, 1942–43 and 1946–48; History Tripos Parts I and II Class II Division I; m. 1949 Rita Thomas (1 s. 1953; 1 d. 1956); R.N.V.R. ordinary seaman June 1943; commissioned April 1944; Sub-Lieutenant; released September 1946; Assistant Principal, Commonwealth Relations Office, 1st November 1948; Peshawar July 1949; Karachi February 1950 to August 1951; Principal (temporary) 1st December 1952; Principal 20th September 1953; Delhi August 1955 to February 1959; Accra June 1962; Acting Deputy High Commissioner June to July 1962; substituting Assistant Secretary 16th June 1962.

WALKER, William Frederick; born 16th April 1921; educated Reay School, London, S.W.9; m. 1945 Lena Walton; military service 1940–46; served in Libya, India, Burma and Germany; Clerical Officer, Board of Trade, August 1946; Executive Officer, Commonwealth Relations Office, August 1955; Kuala Lumpur 31st May 1957 to February 1961 (local Higher Executive Officer); Higher Executive Officer 5th July 1960; Ibadan July 1961.

WALKER-BRASH, John Douglas Grenville; born 31st July 1919; educated Westminster School; military service 1939–51, Infantry; Brigade Major, 77 Brigade, 2nd Chindit Expedition; Mentioned in Despatches; also served in India, Egypt, Palestine, Eritrea, Greece and Malaya; journalist 1951–58; Commentator, Cyprus Broadcasting Service (Government of Cyprus), 1956–59; Temporary Senior Information Officer, Commonwealth Relations Office, 7th December 1959; First Secretary (Information), Accra, December 1959; Acting Director of Information Services, Accra, December 1960 to April 1961 and July to December 1962; substituting Principal Information Officer 11th August 1962.

WALL, Robert Duncan; born 18th March 1912; m. 1939 Jane Dudley (1 d. 1940; 1 s. 1944); Commander, R.N.; Master Mariner; Executive and Air Branches of the Royal Navy 1928–45; Dominions Office December 1945; Assistant Secretary (Information), Ottawa, 1946–49; Director, Information Office, Colombo, 1949–52; First Secretary (Information), Karachi, May to October 1952; Regional Information Officer, Lahore and Peshawar, 1952–54; Ottawa 1955–57; Director, U.K.I.S., Salisbury, March 1958 to April 1961; Principal Information Officer 1st October 1960; Director of Information, Cyprus, September 1961.
WALSH ATKINS, Leonard Brian, CMG (1962), CVO (1961); born 15th March 1915;

educated Charterhouse (Scholar) 1928–34 and Hertford College, Oxford (Scholar) 1934–37; 2nd Class Honours in Lit.Hum.; BA; m. 1940 Marguerite Black (3 s. 1942, 1944, 1949); Assistant Principal, India Office, 11th October 1937; Assistant Secretary to Indian Delegation to League of Nations Assembly September 1938; Fleet Air Arm November 1940; commissioned October 1941; 822 N.A. Squadron, Home Fleet; Ty/A/Lieutenant-Commander (A), and Air Staff Officer, M.V. Adula , Western Approaches, December 1943; Mentioned in Despatches December 1945; released July 1945; Principal ( in absentia ) 1st November 1941; on deputation to Burma 1946, to India and Pakistan 1948; Assistant Secretary 1st March 1949; Counsellor, Dublin, September 1953 to April 1956; Imperial Defence College 1957; Deputy High Commissioner, Karachi, January 1959 to December 1961; Assistant Under-Secretary of State (temporary) 28th May 1962.

WALWYN, Fanny, MBE (1953); born 17th May 1906; educated Heywood (Lancs.) Grammar School 1916–23; S.C. and T., Heywood Post Office, 10th September 1923; Clerical Officer, Money-Order Department, 13th January 1929; India Office 3rd April 1933; Executive Officer 10th November 1941; Higher Executive Officer 31st October 1949.

WATFORD (née Kitchen), Irene Pauline; born 31st March 1911; educated St. Martin's High School, London; m. 1952 Major Ronald Watford (I.A. Retd.); Telephonist, London Telephone Service, 13th November 1928; Clerical Officer, India Office, 30th May 1938; Executive Officer 5th May 1943; Delhi December 1948 to February 1951; Higher Executive Officer 28th May 1951; seconded to Department of Technical Co-operation 1st April 1962.

WATSON, Jean Radcliffe, MA; born 4th September 1925; educated Gateshead Secondary School 1936–43 and Newnham College, Cambridge, (Exhibitioner and Scholar) 1943–47; Modern and Medieval Languages Tripos Part I Class I 1945; Oriental Languages Tripos Part I Class I 1947; Treasury Studentship in Oriental Languages 1948–51; Temporary Assistant Keeper, Class II, India Office Library, 1st October 1951; Assistant Keeper, Class I, 18th June 1954.

WATTS, Rosslyn Reginald Gordon; born 24th November 1929; educated City of Bath Boys' School 1941–48; m. 1951 Ivy Young (1 d. 1955); National Service (R.A.O.C.) 1948–50; 2nd Lieutenant; Executive Officer, Commonwealth Relations Office, 22nd May 1950; Higher Executive Officer 18th October 1954; Dacca March 1955 to September 1957; Lahore to July 1958; Senior Executive Officer 24th August 1960.

WATTS, William John; born 11th March 1923; educated Oakham School 1936–41 and St. Catherine's College, Cambridge, 1946–47; m. 1949 Anne Watt (1 s. 1951; 1 d. 1960); R.A.F. Service 1941–46; P.O.W., Germany, 1943–45; R.A.F. Japanese Course 1945–46; Malayan Civil Service 1948; studied Chinese in Macau 1949–51; Chinese Affairs, Kedah, 1951–53; District Officer 1953–55; Assistant Secretary, External Defence, 1956–57; Deputy Secretary, Ministry of Interior and Justice, 1957–59; resigned from Malayan Government Service August 1959; Temporary Principal, Commonwealth Relations Office, January 1960; Principal 29th December 1961; Colombo November 1962.

WEHL, David Anthony; born 28th December 1905; educated Merchant Taylors' School; Publicity Officer for Italian State Railways in London 1925; Staff Speaker for Ministry of Information 1940; Lieutenant, Royal Engineers, 1943; Major (Deputy Assistant Director, Public Relations), 14th Army, Far East Land Forces, 1945; Information Officer, Central Office of Information, 1947; Senior Information Officer 1951; Deputy Regional Information Officer, Leeds, 1952; Commonwealth Relations Office, 15th August 1958; Regional Information Officer, Penang, 1958; Acting Director, Information Services, Kuala Lumpur 1960; Regional Information Officer, Ibadan, 1961; author of Field Fare , The Goldfish Bowl , The Birth of Indonesia , The Moon Upside Down , Old Moley , Modern Malay Usage ; productions: Torn Banner , And If I Laugh .

WELLINGTON, Sidney; born 30th December 1922; educated Tottenham County School; m. 1947 Lilian Andrews (2 s. 1948, 1952); Clerical Officer, India Office, 30th May 1939; Executive Officer 8th January 1948; Higher Executive Officer 21st December 1955; Salisbury September 1958 to July 1961; Senior Executive Officer 1st May 1962.

WENBAN-SMITH, William Nigel; born 1st September 1936; educated The King's School, Canterbury and King's College, Cambridge; Classical Tripos Class II Division I 1961; m. 1961 Charlotte Chapman-Andrews; National Service (R.N.) 1955–57; Plebiscite Supervisory Officer, Southern Cameroons, August 1960 to February 1961; Assistant Principal, Commonwealth Relations Office, 4th September 1961.

WHITE, Winifred May; born 27th December 1907; educated Blakesley House School, Merton; Typist, India Office, 3rd May 1927; Shorthand-Typist 19th July 1930; Clerical Officer 16th May 1935; Executive Officer 26th June 1944; Higher Executive Officer 22nd August 1960; seconded to Department of Technical Co-operation 24th July 1961.

WHITECROSS, Douglas Michael, born 28th February 1931; educated King Edward's School, Birmingham 1943–46; m. 1961 Yvonne Quinn; Temporary Clerk, Inland Revenue, 1946; Tax Officer 26th June 1947; National Service in R.A.F. September 1949 to September 1951; Commonwealth Relations Office 1st October 1957; Calcutta March 1959 to March 1961; Higher Executive Officer (local) 24th August 1959; seconded to Department of Technical Co-operation 24th July 1961; Higher Executive Officer 29th January 1962.

WHITEHEAD, George Sydney, MVO (1961); born 15th November 1915; educated Boys' County School, Harrow, 1927–34 and London School of Economics (Exhibitioner) 1934–37; BSc (Econ.) 1937; m. 1948 Constance Hart (née Vale) (1 step-d. 1944; 1 d. 1950); Executive Officer, India Office; 12th December 1934; service in R.A. (H.A.A.) June 1940 to September 1945; served in Gold Coast December 1943 to August 1945; Captain; Higher Executive Officer ( in absentia ) July 1940; Private Secretary to Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State December 1945 to December 1946; Principal, Burma Office, December 1946; Rangoon, Burma, October 1947 to January 1948; seconded to Foreign Office (British Embassy, Rangoon) January to April 1948; Canberra May 1952 to July 1955; Assistant Secretary (local), Calcutta, 9th January 1958 to August 1961; Assistant Secretary 1st April 1960; Inspector 13th November 1961.

WICKSON, Dalby Walter Harry, MBE (1951); born 3rd July 1906; educated Bedford Modern School 1918–24; m. 1930 Irene Walduck (3 s. 1932, 1934, 1945; 1 d. 1937); S.C. and T., G.P.O., July 1924; Clerical Officer, Colonial Office, 12th May 1930; Higher Clerical Officer 6th July 1940; served in R.A.S.C. 1942–46; Sergeant; attached to War Office for duty with Malayan Planning Staff August 1944; released March 1946; Staff Officer (later Higher Executive Officer), Dominions Office, 19th February 1947; Passport Officer, Capetown, December 1949 to May 1953; Senior Executive Officer 18th May 1953; Delhi March 1958 to March 1962; Chief Executive Officer (local) 1st July 1961; Chief Executive Officer (temporary) 30th July 1962.

B. Council and Herts and Berks County Councils 1929–37; Certificate, Poor Law Examinations Board, 1936; Social Welfare Officer, Portsmouth City Council, 1937 and Warwickshire County Council 1939–48; Commissioned as Navigator 1943–45; Executive Officer,

WILLCOCKS, Derek; born 14th June 1930; educated Plymouth College and Devonport High School; m. 1956 Sylvia Freeman (1 s. 1957; 2 d. 1959, 1961); National Service with Royal Air Force 1949–51; Executive Officer, Ministry of Supply, June 1951 (seniority from 1st April 1949); Ministry of Aviation October 1959; Higher Executive Officer, Commonwealth Relations Office, 17th October 1960; Karachi February 1962.

WILLEY, Peter; born 12th June 1923; educated Frimley and Camberley County Secondary School 1935–40; m. 1946 Josephine Walsh (1 s. 1961); Temporary Clerk, Ministry of Food, August 1940; Clerical Officer April 1948; Executive Officer, Commonwealth Relations Office, 1st December 1955; Calcutta February 1957 to December 1958; seconded to Department of Technical Co-operation July 1961; Higher Executive Officer May 1962.

WILLIAMS, John Robert; born 15th September 1922; educated Sheen County School and Fitzwilliam House, Cambridge; Geographical Tripos Part I Class II Division I; Archaeological and Anthropological Tripos Part II Class I; m. 1958 Helga Elizabeth Konow Lund (2 d. 1959, 1962; 1 s. 1960); military service 1941–46; enlisted in ranks of King's Royal Rifle Corps; commissioned service with 1st Battalion King's African Rifles in East and Central Africa; Ceylon and Burma campaign; Captain; Assistant Principal, Colonial Office, August 1949; Secretary, Singapore Riots Inquiry Commission, 1951; Principal November 1951; visited Somaliland, Nyasaland and Southern Rhodesia 1954; visited Addis Ababa for negotiation of Anglo/Ethiopian Agreement on Somali Grazing Areas 1954; seconded to Commonwealth Relations Office as Principal; Delhi March 1956; transferred to Commonwealth Relations Office June 1958; Deputy High Commissioner, Penang, August 1959 to October 1962; Kuala Lumpur October 1962.

WINTER, Arnold Martin; born 22nd December 1920; educated Upton House Central School, London; m. 1951 Rita Hillringhaus (1 s. 1951; 1 d. 1956); war service in R.A.F. 1941–46; Temporary Clerk II, Control Commission for Germany, September 1946; Control Officer III 10th May 1948; Clerical Officer, Commonwealth Relations Office, 14th June 1951; Karachi October 1953 to March 1958; Executive Officer 24th
December 1954; Colombo May 1959 to June 1961; Higher Executive Officer 29th January 1962.

WOOL-LEWIS, Cyril Eric, OBE (1951); born 17th June 1906; educated Westminster School and Trinity College, Cambridge, 1925–29; Commissioned in East Surrey Regiment 1929; Assistant Commissioner of Police, Sierra Leone, 1935; Assistant District Commissioner, Sierra Leone, 1937; District Commissioner 1942; Commissioner of the Turks and Caicos Islands, B.W.I., 1946–51; seconded to the Colonial Office 1951–53; Administrative Officer Class II, Northern Nigeria, 1953; Administrative Officer Class I 1954; transferred to Nigerian Federal Government Service 1956; Staff Grade 1956; Permanent Secretary 1957; retired from Overseas Civil Service June 1961; Temporary Principal, Commonwealth Relations Office, October 1961.

WOOLVERTON, Kenneth Arthur Frederick; born 4th August 1926; educated Orange Hill Grammar School 1938–44; m. Kathleen Eleanor West; Meteorologist, R.A.F. 1944–48; Hendon Borough Council 1948–50; Clerical Officer, Colonial Office, 3rd April 1950; Executive Officer 19th February 1952; Higher Executive Officer 11th July 1960; Commonwealth Relations Office 18th December 1961.

WRIGHT, John Arthur, born 10th January 1932; educated Heanor Grammar School, Derbyshire; m. 1956 Enfys Ann Roberts (1s. 1962); Clerical Officer, Colonial Office, May 1948; National Service (Royal Air Force) 1950–52; Librarian IV, Commonwealth Relations Office, September 1959; Librarian III September 1962.

WYTHERS, John Rowland Graham, BEM (1945); born 2nd February 1918; educated Soham Grammar School, Cambs., 1929–34; m. 1945 Joyce Warren (1 s. 1946; 1 d. 1948); Social Welfare Department, London County Council, 1934–39; Relieving Officers' Certificate, Poor Law Examinations Board, 1936; Territorial Army (Royal Artillery, Heavy A.A.) 1939–42; served in Norway and North Africa; transferred to Intelligence Corps 1942; served in Persia and Iraq (Warrant Officer); General Grade Clerk, Social Welfare Department, London County Council, 1946–47; Assistant Area Meals Officer, Civic Restaurants Department, 1st May 1947; Executive Officer, National Assistance Board, 12th July 1948; Higher Executive Officer, Commonwealth Relations Office, 15th December 1958; Chief Registrar 1st January 1959; Bombay December 1959; substituting Senior Executive Officer 26th May to 16th July 1961.
